tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
I miss Kate so badly that I physically hurt. 

It's been a week since we were together. She's still in Australia sorting her visa. 

I feel more stressed. My emotions are stunted. My thoughts are cloudy and my mood has dipped. 

It just hurts everywhere.

tweets.text: @mattkratter BTC Yield measures the increase in BTC per share, not total shareholder accretion. Last week Strategy added ₿1,550 of BTC and $100 million of USD Reserve. When both assets are included, the transaction was accretive to MSTR shareholders.

thread_tweets.text: @mattkratter BTC Yield measures the increase in BTC per share, not total shareholder accretion. Last week Strategy added ₿1,550 of BTC and $100 million of USD Reserve. When both assets are included, the transaction was accretive to MSTR shareholders.
thread_tweets.full_text: @mattkratter BTC Yield measures the increase in BTC per share, not total shareholder accretion. Last week Strategy added ₿1,550 of BTC and $100 million of USD Reserve. When both assets are included, the transaction was accretive to MSTR shareholders.

thread_tweets.text: The document you cite clearly states that BTC Yield is a narrow KPI used to assess per-share accretion solely as it pertains to bitcoin holdings. It is not a measure of financial performance, valuation, liquidity, ROI, book value, or stockholder return. Full financial analysis requires a more comprehensive review of our financial statements and SEC disclosures.
thread_tweets.full_text: The document you cite clearly states that BTC Yield is a narrow KPI used to assess per-share accretion solely as it pertains to bitcoin holdings. It is not a measure of financial performance, valuation, liquidity, ROI, book value, or stockholder return. Full financial analysis requires a more comprehensive review of our financial statements and SEC disclosures.

thread_tweets.text:
Retweets are notifications, not endorsements. When I endorse something, I say so.

Bitcoin-backed credit instruments compete with fiat and crypto yield products, not with Bitcoin. When capital moves from fiat/crypto collateral into BTC-backed instruments, it strengthens the Bitcoin network.

tweets.full_text:
@mattkratter BTC Yield measures the increase in BTC per share, not total shareholder accretion. Last week Strategy added ₿1,550 of BTC and $100 million of USD Reserve. When both assets are included, the transaction was accretive to MSTR shareholders.

The document you cite clearly states that BTC Yield is a narrow KPI used to assess per-share accretion solely as it pertains to bitcoin holdings. It is not a measure of financial performance, valuation, liquidity, ROI, book value, or stockholder return. Full financial analysis requires a more comprehensive review of our financial statements and SEC disclosures.

tweets.text:
When you look at whats going on socially in America and Europe it feels like we're being driven into seeing everything black and white (no pun intended) and the people that see things as more of a grey, which in reality  tend to be correct most of the time, those ppl are either staying quiet for fear of upsetting both sides or being drowned out by the louder voices because due to our tribalistic nature it feels good to be on the battlefield.

What's going on this week reminds me in a way of the tipping point reached when the George Floyd video was released. Ppl forget just days before there was a video of a black man in central park minding his business walking his dog and this white woman called the cops and said he was trying to hurt her, it was bad. Ppl were really upset when that came out and u combine that with the stress of an early covid shutdown and the country became a powder keg.

In Europe we just had Henry Nowak and that horrible beheading in succession and in America Belton and Anthony cases. It's weird because out of the two personally I think that Belton's shooter not being charged is by far the worse miscarriage of justice when in comparison to ppl yelling free Karmelo. 

Anyway we see whats going on in Ireland and GB right now and I wouldn't be surprised to see something similar happen in Ameirca soon. Combine all this tension with looming ww3+ai threats and its arguably an even worse societal climate than what we experienced in 2020

I don't really have the answers, that needs to come from leadership on both sides but at the very least I guess if you see things grey you gotta speak up. Be safe and be good to each other we gotta find a way to make this work because realistically what is the alternatives here?
